scope.summary: Implement a turntable capture sequence that drives the camera animation track through a configurable 360-degree orbit, captures a frame at each step via ClothViewport::capture_frame, and packages the result as an ordered PNG sequence artifact with a manifest
acceptance_criteria: tailor_capture_turntable Tauri command accepts garment_id, simulation_run_id, step_count (default 36), elevation_deg (default 15), render_mode, and output_dir
The command drives the CameraUniform azimuth from 0 to 360 degrees in step_count equal steps at the specified elevation, capturing one frame per step
All captured frames are written to output_dir as turntable_NNN.png (zero-padded) and registered as a bundle artifact via write_dir_artifact
A TailorTurntableCaptureCompleted EventLedger event is emitted with frame_count, step_deg, garment_id, simulation_run_id, and artifact_id
The tailor_capture_turntable MCP tool wraps the Tauri command and is accessible to model agents
Lighting rig parameters (ambient_color, key_light_direction, key_light_intensity, fill_light_intensity) are exposed in a TurntableLightingRig struct defaulting to a three-point lighting setup
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: run turntable at 8 steps; inspect first and last frame; assert azimuth difference is 315 degrees (each step = 45 degrees)
scope.risk_if_missed: Without turntable capture, evaluating garment quality from all angles requires manually calling tailor_capture_frame at each azimuth. Model agents cannot produce presentation-ready 360 preview sets without custom orchestration. This is a standard operator and production-bridge deliverable.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
